target: Batch: listOfWinIds helper extraction (ISS-2321) + SC_MON_HARDCODED_IDS slash-format (ISS-2320) + contrast paragraph wording (ISS-2323)
summary: Three follow-ups from SEC-part2b-contrast-paragraph c1 review: (1) extracted listOfWinIds(predicate, wins, opts) as a shared utility function near top of generate-html.js (ISS-2321); (2) rewrote scMonHardcodedIds and all 6 caNone*Ids to use the helper — scMonHardcodedIds now uses {sep:'/', prefix:''} producing slash-joined bare IDs e.g. '002/012/029...' (ISS-2320); (3) updated sections.json prose to use WIN-{{SC_MON_HARDCODED_IDS}} with the bare-ID format, and fixed the opening clause ambiguity from 'The contrast with the hardcoded WINs' to 'Within the Self-Contradicted cohort' (ISS-2323). ISS-2322 (adjacent stale live-fetch paragraph) handled separately — spawned ISS-2383 as a tracked follow-up rather than fixing inline here.

patches.description: Extract listOfWinIds(predicate, wins, opts) helper function after first escapeHtml — opts.sep defaults to ', ', opts.prefix defaults to 'WIN-' (ISS-2321)
Rewrite scMonHardcodedIds to use listOfWinIds with {sep:'/', prefix:''} — produces 'NNN/NNN/...' bare-ID slash-joined format per EXP-442 spec (ISS-2320)
Rewrite all 6 caNone*Ids context properties to use listOfWinIds helper (comma-space default, 'WIN-' prefix) — closes the 7-site inline duplication (ISS-2321 bonus)

Update contrast paragraph: (a) change ({{SC_MON_HARDCODED_IDS}}) to (WIN-{{SC_MON_HARDCODED_IDS}}) to account for bare-ID format; (b) rephrase opening clause to 'Within the Self-Contradicted cohort, the monitoring split is even more revealing' (ISS-2320, ISS-2323)

tldr_evidence: Verified: scMonHardcodedIds was using .map(w=>'WIN-'+w.id).join(', ') — comma-space, full prefix. EXP-442 spec required slash-joined bare IDs. caNone*Ids had 6 separate inline copies of the same pattern. listOfWinIds is now a shared utility. Build passes, 3769 tests pass.
tldr_verdict: No verdict change — formatting and code quality improvement. The SC_MON_HARDCODED_IDS rendering now matches EXP-442's original authoring intent.
notes: ISS-2322 (stale live-fetch paragraph) deferred to ISS-2383 — requires template work that extends beyond batch scope.
